name: Bakr ibn Hayy
title: The Recovered Lover
shortDescription: A force in Umar Sa'd's army who, upon seeing the Imam's righteousness, returned to the embrace of Wilayah and was martyred on Ashura.
fullStory:
Bakr ibn Hayy al-Taymi was a resident of Kufa who initially, influenced by the toxic propaganda and pressures of Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad, set out for Karbala with Umar Sa'd's army to fight Imam Hussain (AS).

In the days before Ashura, Bakr witnessed the Imam's conversations with the Kufan army. The Imam's solid logic, the innocence of the Ahl al-Bayt, and the cruel-heartedness of the Kufan commanders awakened his dormant conscience. On the night of Ashura, he made a brave decision.

Bakr ibn Hayy secretly left the army of oppression and joined the camp of light. With tearful eyes, he stood before Imam Hussain (AS) and repented for his past. The Imam kindly accepted his repentance and placed him among his companions.

On the morning of Ashura, Bakr, who had now become a devoted soldier for the Master of Martyrs (AS), stood manfully in the all-out attack by Umar Sa'd's army (the first attack). He wielded his sword to compensate for his past mistake with his own blood.

Finally, after a heroic battle, this repentant of Karbala was martyred by the Kufans' arrows and sword strikes, achieving a blessed end.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى بَكْرِ بْنِ حَيِّ التَّيْمِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Bakr ibn Hayy al-Taymi.
